food.label: Allowed
food.limit: Public edible planting, ripe fruit, nuts, and produce are free to harvest for personal use. Take a fair share, leave some for others, and don't damage the plants or pick rented community-garden plots.
food.note: Bronx River Foodway is a public food forest/edible park with an open community-harvest policy. Geocoded and policy-confirmed June 2026.

medicine.label: Allowed
medicine.limit: Medicinal plantings at this site are part of the open-harvest invitation; harvest gently and take only what is abundant.
medicine.note: Harvest is from cultivated, intentionally planted stock, not wild medicinal foraging. Confirm with the managing organization.

default.label: Unknown
default.limit: Food harvesting is invited here, but collection of craft, fiber, or other non-food plant material is not addressed.
default.note: Ask the managing organization before collecting non-food material ("take food, not the forest").

default.limit: Food harvesting is invited here, but collection of craft, fiber, or other non-food plant material is not addressed.
default.note: Ask the managing organization before collecting non-food material ("take food, not the forest").

food.label: Allowed
food.limit: Public edible planting, ripe fruit, nuts, and produce are free to harvest for personal use. Take a fair share, leave some for others, and don't damage the plants or pick rented community-garden plots.

food.label: Allowed
food.limit: Open harvest year-round for anyone: take what you need of what's ripe and abundant, and leave some for others. Do not pick from the P-patch plots (stone-walled rectangles/hexagons, rented by individual gardeners) or the food bank plot.
food.note: A 7-acre public food forest on Seattle Public Utilities land beside Jefferson Park, founded on an open-harvest, fair-share-for-all policy.
food.sourceLabel: Beacon Food Forest open harvest policy

medicine.label: Allowed
medicine.limit: The open-harvest invitation covers the site's medicinal plantings: harvest gently, take only what's abundant, and leave some for others. Do not pick from P-patch plots or the food bank plot.
medicine.note: Please leave the sust̓əlǰixʷali Traditional Indian Medicine garden, tended by the Seattle Indian Health Board, to its caretakers unless invited.
medicine.sourceLabel: Beacon Food Forest open harvest policy

default.label: Allowed
default.limit: The open-harvest invitation extends to the site's crafting plants: harvest gently, take only what's abundant, and leave some for others. Do not pick from P-patch plots or the food bank plot.
default.note: Beacon Food Forest describes more than 100 edible, medicinal, and crafting plants open for community harvest.
default.sourceLabel: Beacon Food Forest open harvest policy

rules.name: Dr. George Washington Carver Edible Park

food.label: Allowed
food.area: Dr. George Washington Carver Edible Park (Asheville)
food.limit: Visitors are encouraged to enjoy freshly picked produce from the park's 40+ fruit and nut species, and discouraged from taking more than their fair share.
food.note: The nation's first public food forest (1997), a City of Asheville park maintained with Bountiful Cities. Leave the community-garden plots to their gardeners.

default.label: Unknown
default.area: Dr. George Washington Carver Edible Park (Asheville)
default.limit: The city's open invitation covers picking produce to eat; collection of craft or medicinal material is not addressed.
default.note: Ask Bountiful Cities or Asheville Parks & Recreation before collecting non-food material.

food.label: Allowed
food.limit: Open foraging of fruits, vegetables, and herbs, free for the taking: gather only what's abundant and healthy, take only what you need, and leave some to reseed.
food.note: A fence-less public food forest on Austin parkland beside the Festival Beach Community Garden, don't pick from the garden's individual plots. Double-check identification; not every plant here is edible.

medicine.label: Allowed
medicine.limit: The food forest grows medicinal herbs as part of its public plantings, with signage on which plants are medicinal: harvest gently and take only what you need.
medicine.note: Confirm identification against the site's signage, and leave enough for the plant to recover and others to share.

default.label: Unknown
default.limit: The open-foraging invitation covers food and medicinal herbs; collection of craft material is not addressed, "take food, not the forest."
default.note: Ask the food forest stewards before collecting non-food material.

rules.name: Urban Food Forest at Browns Mill

food.label: Allowed
food.area: Urban Food Forest at Browns Mill (Atlanta)
food.limit: The City of Atlanta's public food forest grows nuts, fruits, vegetables, herbs, and mushrooms for public consumption; harvest modestly and follow posted guidance and any volunteer-led harvest practices.
food.note: The nation's largest public food forest, community-managed with excess harvests shared with local food pantries. The city's detailed harvest-guidelines page is currently offline, follow on-site signage.

default.label: Unknown
default.area: Urban Food Forest at Browns Mill (Atlanta)
default.limit: The city's public-consumption invitation covers food; collection of craft or medicinal material is not addressed.
default.note: Ask the AgLanta team or on-site stewards before collecting non-food material.

default.label: Prohibited
default.limit: No harvesting or collection of any plant material on Monticello property, including the Saunders-Monticello Trail.
default.note: Monticello is a private historic museum property; its gardens and grounds are protected landscapes.

food.label: Allowed
food.limit: Ripe fruits, nuts, and herbs in the pavilion gardens are open for respectful picking and consumption when the gardens are not in use for events.
food.note: UVA's garden teams invite tasting of ripe produce; do not pick unripe fruit or clip branches.

medicine.label: Allowed
medicine.limit: Ripe herbs may be picked for personal consumption; do not clip branches or take more than a meal's worth.
medicine.note: UVA's invitation covers edible consumption of ripe garden produce; treat medicinal gathering as the same light, respectful tasting.

default.label: Unknown
default.limit: UVA's open-picking invitation covers ripe edible produce; collection of craft material is not addressed.
default.note: Ask UVA's gardens staff before collecting non-food material.
